title: 10 best herbal teas for toothache
intro_desc: Herbal teas for toothache are natural remedies made from various plants and herbs that can help alleviate toothache symptoms. They work by reducing inflammation, numbing the pain, and promoting overall oral health. Some examples of herbal teas that help with toothache include chamomile, clove, and peppermint teas. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease pain. Clove tea contains eugenol, a natural anesthetic that can numb the pain. Peppermint tea has antiseptic and analgesic properties that can soothe inflamed gums and provide relief from toothache. By using herbal teas for toothache, individuals can find natural and effective relief from toothache symptoms, improving their quality of life and overall oral health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow tea helps with toothache because it contains salicin, a compound that has similar properties to aspirin. Salicin works as a natural painkiller and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and alleviating pain in the affected area. Additionally, white willow tea has antioxidant and antibacterial properties, which can help fight infection and promote healing. Drinking white willow tea may provide relief from toothache symptoms and support overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White willow tea contains salicin, which is converted to salicylic acid in the body, reducing inflammation and alleviating toothache pain.
Analgesic: The salicin in white willow tea acts as a natural painkiller, helping to relieve the discomfort caused by toothaches.
Antipyretic: White willow tea can help reduce fever, which is often associated with toothaches, thanks to its salicin content.
Astringent: White willow tea has tannins that can help constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling, providing relief for toothache.
Antibacterial: White willow tea has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat toothache-causing bacteria and promote oral health.
remedy_recipe: In a mortar and pestle, grind 2 teaspoons of dried white willow bark into a powder.
Place the powder in a tea infuser and put it in a teapot.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the tea infuser.
Let the tea steep for 10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser and drink the tea, avoiding the use of sweeteners.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the white willow plant contains salicin, which is a natural pain reliever and can help alleviate toothache.
Leaves: The leaves of the white willow plant also contain salicin and can be used to make medicinal teas for toothache.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the white willow plant contain high levels of salicin and are often used in medicinal teas for their anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Quaking aspen tea helps with toothache because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the mouth. The bark of the aspen tree has been used traditionally to treat various ailments, including toothaches. The tea is made from the inner bark of the tree and has been found to contain compounds such as salicin, which is a natural pain reliever. Drinking aspen tea may help to soothe toothaches and provide relief until you can see a dentist. However, it is important to note that while herbal remedies can provide temporary relief, they should not be used as a substitute for professional dental care.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Quaking aspen tea reduces inflammation, which is important for toothache as it helps alleviate pain and swelling associated with tooth-related inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties come from compounds such as salicin and flavonoids.
Analgesic: The tea has pain-relieving properties that help manage toothache, thanks to the presence of salicin, a natural analgesic.

Antimicrobial: Quaking aspen tea inhibits the growth of bacteria, which is crucial for toothache as it can help prevent and treat infections causing tooth pain. The antimicrobial properties are attributed to compounds like tannins and phenolic acids.

Antioxidant: The tea contains antioxidants that support overall oral health and help reduce toothache by fighting free radicals and promoting tissue healing. The antioxidant properties are due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Astringent: Quaking aspen tea has astringent properties that help reduce inflammation and control bleeding, making it helpful for toothache caused by gum issues. The astringent properties are primarily due to tannins present in the tea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 quaking aspen (Populus tremuloides) leaves, ensuring they are fully grown and healthy.
Rinse leaves under cold water to remove dirt and insects.
Crush or tear leaves into small pieces, releasing their essential oils.
Steep crushed leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea, discarding the leaves, and drink up to three times a day for toothache relief.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the quaking aspen contains salicin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate toothache.
Buds: The buds of the quaking aspen are rich in essential oils and phenolic acids, making them a good choice for medicinal teas to soothe toothache.
Leaves: The leaves of the quaking aspen contain flavonoids and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can help reduce toothache.
Stems: The stems of the quaking aspen contain salicin and other compounds that have pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for medicinal teas for toothache.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with toothache because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the affected area. The tea is made from the flowers, leaves, and roots of the yarrow plant, which contain compounds that can help numb the pain and promote healing. Yarrow tea has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and fight infection. Drinking yarrow tea or using it as a mouthwash may help soothe toothache and promote oral health. However, it is important to note that yarrow tea is not a substitute for professional dental care, and it is always best to consult with a dentist for any dental issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of flavonoids, helps reduce swelling and pain in the gums and tooth.
Analgesic: The analgesic effect of yarrow tea, caused by volatile oils, can alleviate toothache and provide temporary relief.
Antispasmodic: Yarrow tea's antispasmodic property, resulting from lactones, can help soothe muscle spasms and ease toothache.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property of yarrow tea, due to the presence of tannins, can help kill bacteria causing tooth decay and infection.
Astringent: Yarrow tea's astringent property, caused by tannins, can help reduce bleeding and shrink inflamed gums, thus reducing toothache.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh yarrow leaves and flowers.
Rinse the herbs under cold water, then chop them coarsely.
Place the chopped herbs in a teapot and add 2 cups of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 15 minutes, then strain out the herbs.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day.
remedy_parts: Roots: Yarrow roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce toothache pain.
Leaves: Yarrow leaves have antimicrobial properties that can help fight tooth infections causing toothache.
Flowers: Yarrow flowers have analgesic properties that can help alleviate toothache pain.
Buds: Yarrow buds are rich in antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation and provide relief from toothache.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with toothache because it has antibacterial and analgesic properties that can alleviate pain and reduce inflammation. Thyme contains compounds such as thymol, which is a natural antiseptic that can help kill the bacteria causing toothache. Drinking thyme tea or using thyme oil as a mouthwash can help soothe the pain and promote oral health. Additionally, thyme tea has calming effects that can help reduce stress and anxiety associated with toothache. Drinking thyme tea regularly can also help prevent tooth decay and gum diseases, thus reducing the risk of toothache.
remedy_properties: Analgesic: Thyme tea has pain-relieving properties that help alleviate toothache by reducing the sensation of pain. This is due to the presence of active constituents such as thymol and carvacrol.

Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, providing relief from toothache. This is due to the presence of active constituents such as flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Antibacterial: Thyme tea has antibacterial properties that help kill the bacteria causing tooth decay and infection, providing relief from toothache. This is due to the presence of active constituents such as thymol and carvacrol.

Antioxidant: Thyme tea has antioxidant properties that help protect the gums and teeth from damage caused by free radicals, promoting oral health and reducing the risk of toothache. This is due to the presence of active constituents such as flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Anesthetic: Thyme tea has anesthetic properties that help numb the affected area, providing relief from toothache. This is due to the presence of active constituents such as thymol and carvacrol.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried thyme leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the leaves.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before sipping slowly, holding it in the mouth near the affected tooth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in flavonoids and volatile oils, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help alleviate toothache.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain essential oils and bioactive compounds, which can help reduce inflammation and fight oral bacteria, providing relief from toothache.
Stems: Thyme stems have therapeutic compounds that can help soothe toothache by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tea helps with toothache because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the mouth. The rosmarinic acid present in rosemary has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause dental infections, thereby promoting oral health. Additionally, rosemary tea can help to reduce inflammation in the gums, which can help to alleviate toothache and promote overall oral health. Drinking rosemary tea can also help to soothe a sore throat and reduce fever, which can often accompany a toothache.
remedy_properties: Analgesic: Rosemary tea can help alleviate toothache by acting as a natural painkiller, thanks to the presence of active constituents such as rosmarinic acid and carnosol.

Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums and mouth, providing relief from toothache, due to the presence of compounds like camphor and eucalyptol.

Antibacterial: Rosemary tea can inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for tooth decay and infections, thus helping to manage toothache, attributed to the presence of compounds like caffeic acid and rosmarinic acid.

Antioxidant: Its high antioxidant content helps protect gum tissue from damage caused by free radicals, promoting oral health and reducing toothache, due to the presence of powerful antioxidants like carnosol and rosmarinic acid.

Circulatory stimulant: Rosemary tea promotes blood flow to the gums, which aids in delivering essential nutrients and oxygen to the affected area, thereby helping to alleviate toothache, due to the presence of active constituents like α-pinene and 1,8-cineole.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine one cup of water and two sprigs of fresh rosemary.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Turn off the heat and allow the mixture to steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain out the rosemary, leaving only the liquid.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day as needed.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the rosemary plant are rich in essential oils and have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective for alleviating toothache.
Flowers: The flowers of the rosemary plant contain flavonoids and have antioxidant properties, making them useful for reducing inflammation and providing pain relief for toothache.
Stems: The stems of the rosemary plant contain rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, making them beneficial for treating toothache.
Seeds: The seeds of the rosemary plant are rich in essential oils, which have antimicrobial properties, making them useful for preventing tooth decay and alleviating toothache.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tea helps with toothache because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the mouth. The active compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and reduce inflammation. Drinking lavender tea can help soothe irritated gums and provide relief from toothache. Additionally, lavender tea has a calming effect, which can help reduce anxiety and stress associated with tooth pain. However, it is important to note that lavender tea should not be used as a substitute for proper dental care and treatment. If you are experiencing severe or persistent toothache, it is important to consult with a dental professional.
remedy_properties: Analgesic: English lavender tea has pain-relieving properties that can help alleviate toothache by reducing the sensation of pain. This is due to the presence of linalool and linalyl acetate, which are known to have analgesic effects.

Anti-inflammatory: Lavender tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums and mouth, thus providing relief from toothache. The active constituents responsible for this property include cineole and camphor.

Antibacterial: English lavender tea has antibacterial properties that can help kill the bacteria causing tooth decay and infection, thereby providing relief from toothache. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins, flavonoids, and terpenoids.

Anxiolytic: Lavender tea has calming and relaxing properties that can help reduce anxiety and stress associated with toothache. This is due to the presence of linalool and linalyl acetate, which are known to have anxiolytic effects.

Antispasmodic: English lavender tea has antispasmodic properties that can help alleviate muscle spasms and tension in the jaw and face, thus providing relief from toothache. The active constituents responsible for this property include cineole and camphor.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, pour 1 cup of water and bring it to a boil.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried English lavender flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the lavender flowers.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature before using it as a mouthwash for toothache.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, the flowers of English lavender have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate toothache.
Leaves: Containing volatile oils, lavender leaves can help reduce inflammation and provide pain relief for toothaches.
Buds: High in antioxidants, lavender buds can help soothe toothaches and reduce oral inflammation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with toothache because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the affected area. The tea's antiviral and antibacterial properties may also help fight off any infection causing the toothache. Moreover, lemon balm contains eugenol, a compound known for its numbing effect, which can help relieve tooth pain. Drinking lemon balm tea or using it as a mouthwash can provide a soothing effect and offer relief from toothache.
remedy_properties: Analgesic property: Lemon balm tea has pain-relieving abilities that can help alleviate toothache by reducing the sensation of pain. This property is important for toothache because it provides immediate relief from the discomfort caused by the toothache. The active constituents responsible for this property are rosmarinic acid and citronellal.

Anti-inflammatory property: Lemon balm tea has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums and mouth. This property is important for toothache because it targets the root cause of the pain and helps to reduce the inflammation that is causing the toothache. The active constituents responsible for this property are eugenol and rosmarinic acid.

Antibacterial property: Lemon balm tea has antibacterial properties that can help eliminate the bacteria causing the toothache. This property is important for toothache because it helps to prevent the infection from spreading and worsening the toothache. The active constituents responsible for this property are citral and citronellal.

Anxiolytic property: Lemon balm tea has calming and relaxing effects that can help reduce anxiety and stress related to toothache. This property is important for toothache because it can help to alleviate the emotional distress caused by the toothache and promote relaxation. The active constituents responsible for this property are rosmarinic acid and citronellal.

Antispasmodic property: Lemon balm tea has antispasmodic effects that can help relax the muscles and reduce spasms in the mouth and jaw. This property is important for toothache because it can help to alleviate the tension and discomfort caused by the toothache. The active constituents responsible for this property are rosmarinic acid and citronellal.
remedy_recipe: Pick 2 tablespoons of fresh lemon balm leaves or use 1 tablespoon of dried leaves.
Rinse the lemon balm leaves under cold water to remove any dirt or impurities.
Crush the lemon balm leaves gently to release their oils and flavors.
Steep the crushed lemon balm leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the lemon balm tea and allow it to cool before using as a mouthwash for toothache.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are commonly used in medicinal teas for toothache due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Flowers: Lemon balm flowers are used in teas for toothache because of their soothing and calming effects on inflamed gums.
Stems: Although not as potent as the leaves or flowers, lemon balm stems can still be used in teas for toothache, providing a mild anti-inflammatory effect.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano tea helps with toothache because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the affected area. The tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help fight off infection and promote healing. Additionally, oregano oil, which is derived from the oregano plant, has been shown to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help kill off the bacteria that cause tooth decay and gum disease. Drinking oregano tea can help soothe a toothache and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Oregano tea's anti-inflammatory property, due to its rosmarinic acid and thymol content, helps reduce toothache-related swelling and inflammation.
Analgesic: The analgesic effect of oregano tea, caused by its carvacrol and thymol constituents, aids in relieving toothache pain.
Antibacterial: Oregano tea's antibacterial property, attributed to its thymol and carvacrol components, assists in fighting tooth infection and preventing tooth decay.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant property of oregano tea, owing to its rosmarinic acid and thymol content, helps protect oral tissues from damage and supports overall oral health.
Anesthetic: Oregano tea's anesthetic property, resulting from its thymol and carvacrol constituents, contributes to numbing toothache-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Crush 2 teaspoons of dried oregano leaves in a mortar and pestle.
Place the crushed oregano leaves in a tea infuser or strainer.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the oregano in a teapot or cup.
Allow the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the oregano leaves and drink the tea, gently swishing it around the mouth before swallowing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: High in flavonoids and phenolic acids, oregano leaves are often used in medicinal teas for toothache due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Flowers: Packed with antioxidants, oregano flowers can be used in teas to help reduce inflammation and provide pain relief for toothaches.
Seeds: Rich in essential oils, oregano seeds can be steeped in hot water to create a medicinal tea that helps alleviate toothache pain and fight infection.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Winter savory tea helps with toothache because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help to reduce pain and swelling in the affected area. The tea is made from the leaves of the winter savory plant, which contain high levels of antioxidants that help to fight off infection and promote healing. Additionally, winter savory tea has antibacterial properties that help to kill the bacteria that cause tooth decay and gum disease, which can contribute to toothache. Drinking winter savory tea can provide natural relief for toothache and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Analgesic: Winter savory tea has pain-relieving properties that help alleviate toothache by reducing the sensation of pain, due to the presence of volatile oils such as carvacrol and thymol.

Anti-inflammatory: The tea's ability to reduce inflammation helps decrease swelling and pain in the affected tooth area, thanks to the presence of active constituents like rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.

Antibacterial: Winter savory tea inhibits the growth of bacteria responsible for tooth decay and infections, making it effective in treating toothaches, attributed to its rich content of phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: The tea's high antioxidant properties help protect gum tissue from damage caused by toothache, as well as promote oral health, due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and other polyphenols.
Anesthetic: Winter savory tea has a mild numbing effect, providing temporary relief from toothache, which is attributed to its essential oils and phenolic compounds.
remedy_recipe: Crush 2 teaspoons of dried winter savory leaves in a mortar and pestle.
Place the crushed leaves in a tea infuser and put it in a teapot.
Boil 2 cups of water and pour it over the tea infuser.
Let the tea steep for 10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser and enjoy the warm herbal tea.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Winter savory leaves are rich in anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them ideal for soothing toothache pain.
Flowers: The flowers of winter savory contain high levels of antioxidants and have been used in medicinal teas to help reduce inflammation and alleviate toothache symptoms.
Seeds: Winter savory seeds are a great source of essential oils, which have natural anesthetic properties that can help numb toothache pain.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's Wort tea helps with toothache because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the affected area. The tea is made from the flowers, leaves, and stems of the St. John's Wort plant, which contains several active compounds, including hypericin and hyperforin. These compounds work together to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, chemicals that cause inflammation and pain in the body. Additionally, St. John's Wort tea has antibacterial properties that can help to prevent infection and promote healing in the affected tooth or gum. Drinking St. John's Wort tea can provide natural relief for toothache and promote oral health.
remedy_properties: Analgesic property: St. John's wort tea can help alleviate toothache pain due to the presence of active constituents like hypericin and hyperforin.
Anti-inflammatory property: The tea reduces inflammation in the affected area, thereby providing relief from toothache, thanks to the anti-inflammatory properties of hypericin and hyperforin.
Antibacterial property: St. John's wort tea helps fight toothache-causing bacteria, as hypericin and hyperforin exhibit antibacterial properties.
Antiviral property: The tea can help combat viral infections that may cause toothache, owing to the antiviral properties of hypericin and pseudohypericin.

Nervine property: St. John's wort tea has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate toothache-related anxiety and discomfort, thanks to the presence of active constituents like hypericin and hyperforin.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 2 teaspoons of dried St. John's Wort herb.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the herbs.
Allow the tea to cool until it is warm, but not hot.
Rinse your mouth with the tea, swishing it around your teeth and gums for several minutes before spitting it out.
remedy_parts: Flowers: St. John's Wort flowers are commonly used in medicinal teas for toothache due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Leaves: The leaves of St. John's Wort contain compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation, making them useful for toothache relief.

Aerial parts: While not a specific part, it's worth noting that aerial parts of St. John's Wort are sometimes used in teas for toothache, as they contain similar pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory compounds as the flowers and leaves.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat toothache with herbal teas is by using a strong tea made from cloves or chamomile. Soak a cotton ball in the cooled tea and apply it directly to the affected tooth. These herbs have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help alleviate pain and reduce swelling. Drinking the tea can also provide internal relief. However, it's important to note that while herbal teas can help manage symptoms, they are not a substitute for professional dental care. If the pain persists, you should consult a dentist.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to toothache that are treated with herbal teas include sore throats and mouth ulcers. For a sore throat, teas made from slippery elm, licorice root, or chamomile can provide relief due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Meanwhile, mouth ulcers can be treated with teas containing calendula or echinacea, which have antibacterial and healing effects. It is important to note that while these herbal teas can help alleviate symptoms, they should not replace proper medical attention when necessary. Always consult with a healthcare professional for appropriate treatment.
